slides.title: Your brain is starving for *one fat*.
slides.sub: Most people get almost none of it, and they feel the gap in their mood and focus.

slides.title: The ratio is off
slides.body: Modern diets run heavy in omega-6 and low in omega-3. That *lopsided ratio* can drive inflammation.

slides.title: Eat the fatty fish
slides.body: *Fatty fish twice a week* (salmon, sardines, mackerel) is the most reliable way to close the gap.

slides.title: Feed the gray matter
slides.body: Your brain is mostly fat, and omega-3 may *support mood and focus*. Research suggests the supply matters.

slides.title: It reaches further
slides.body: Beyond the brain, omega-3 *may calm inflammation* showing up in skin and joints.

slides.title: Plants give you some
slides.body: Walnuts, chia, and flax provide *a weaker plant form*. Fish or an algae source is still better.

slides.title: Close the *omega-3 gap*.
slides.body: Snap your meals and Vibe Health logs your food, so you can see how often fatty fish actually lands on your plate.

caption:
Your brain is starving for one fat, and most people get almost none of it. 🐟

Modern diets run heavy in omega-6, so eat fatty fish twice a week. Omega-3 may support mood and focus, and walnuts and flax give a weaker plant form. Save this. 🔖

Snap your meals and Vibe Health logs your food for you → link in bio.
youtube_title: The One Fat Your Brain Is Starving For

An ordinary person cooking a fillet of salmon in a dim home kitchen, steam rising, harsh side light on a focused everyday face.
